metadata.title: Rename.Tools - Free Online Batch File Renaming Tool | No Upload, Privacy-First, Local Processing
metadata.description: Free browser-based batch file renaming tool with regex, sequences, and rule chains. Rename thousands of files instantly with live preview. 100% local processing - no uploads, no tracking, complete privacy guaranteed.

app.title: Rename.Tools | Online Batch File Rename Tool - Free, Fast & Private
app.description: Rename thousands of files instantly in your browser with regex, sequences, and rule chains. Real-time preview, undo/redo support, metadata extraction. 100% local processing - your files never leave your device. Free forever.

about.title: About - Rename.Tools
about.description: Learn about Rename.Tools, the browser-based batch file renaming tool built with Next.js, React, and modern web technologies.

features.title: Features - Rename.Tools
features.description: Explore all features: regex patterns, sequences, case conversion, custom JavaScript, metadata extraction, and more.

guides.title: Guides - Rename.Tools
guides.description: Practical guides for batch file renaming with Rename.Tools: regex, sequences, photo organization, music libraries, and video filenames.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy - Rename.Tools
privacy.description: Our privacy policy: We don't collect any data. All file operations run locally in your browser.

terms.title: Terms of Service - Rename.Tools
terms.description: Terms of service for using Rename.Tools batch file renaming tool.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer - Rename.Tools
disclaimer.description: Important disclaimer and liability information for Rename.Tools users.

home.heroTitle:
Rename thousands of files.
Right in your browser.
home.heroDescription: Combine regex, sequences, and case conversion into powerful rule chains. Every operation runs locally in your browser — your files never leave your device.
home.highlightTitle:
Everything you need,
nothing you don't
home.highlightDesc: A focused set of capabilities designed to make batch renaming fast, safe, and predictable.
home.hl1Desc: Combine 7 rule types — find & replace, regex, sequences, case conversion, insert, remove, and custom JS — in any order
home.hl2Desc: Every change updates instantly as you type. See original names, new names, and conflicts in real time
home.hl3Desc: Automatically spots duplicate filenames before you rename, preventing accidental overwrites
home.hl4Desc: Files never leave your device. No uploads, no server processing, no data retention — close the tab and everything is gone
home.hl5Desc: Built-in undo/redo support, plus export as Bash or PowerShell scripts for offline execution or rollback
home.hl6Desc: No sign-up, no trials, no paywalls. Fully transparent code on GitHub, community-driven
home.ctaTitle: Stop renaming files one by one.
home.ctaDesc: Rename.Tools gives you the power to rename thousands of files with precision and confidence. No sign-up, no downloads, no uploads.
home.ctaSubtext: Free forever • No sign-up • Works offline
home.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about Rename.Tools and how it helps you manage file names at scale.
home.faq1A: Yes, completely free and open source. There are no premium tiers, no trials, and no hidden fees. The full source code is available on GitHub under the AGPL-3.0 license.
home.faq2Q: Do my files get uploaded to a server?
home.faq2A: No. Rename.Tools runs entirely in your browser. Your files are accessed through the File System Access API and never leave your device. There is no server-side processing whatsoever.
home.faq3Q: Why is it safe to use?
home.faq3A: Rename.Tools is architecturally incapable of accessing your files from a server. All processing happens locally in your browser via the File System Access API. No data is transmitted, stored, or logged. The code is fully open source so you can verify this yourself. Plus, built-in undo and live preview ensure you can always review and revert changes before they happen.
home.faq4A: Rename.Tools works best in Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Edge, Brave, Arc) which support the File System Access API for direct renaming. In other browsers, you can use sample test mode or export rename scripts.
home.faq5Q: How many files can it handle?
home.faq5A: Rename.Tools can handle thousands of files in a single batch. The live preview updates in real time even with large file sets. Performance depends on your browser and device capabilities.

useCases.title: Perfect for any workflow
useCases.desc: Whether you're organizing photos, managing media libraries, or cleaning up project files — Rename.Tools adapts to your needs.

photos.title: Organize thousands of photos in seconds
photos.desc: Rename vacation photos with dates, add location prefixes, or create consistent naming from camera files like DSC_0001.jpg to meaningful names.

videos.title: Manage video files with ease
videos.desc: Clean up downloaded video names, add episode numbers to series, or standardize your media library naming conventions.

music.title: Organize your music library
music.desc: Format track names, add artist prefixes, clean up downloaded filenames, or prepare files for media servers.

code.title: Refactor file structures
code.desc: Batch rename components, convert naming conventions (camelCase to kebab-case), or prepare assets for deployment.

archives.title: Tame your document chaos
archives.desc: Add date prefixes, remove version suffixes, standardize naming across departments, or prepare files for archival.

features.heroDesc: From powerful rule chains to intelligent automation, explore every capability that makes Rename.Tools the most complete browser-based batch renaming tool.

rules.title:
7 rule types.
Combine them freely.
rules.desc: Build complex rename operations by chaining multiple rules. Each rule processes in order — mix find & replace, regex, sequences, case conversion, insert, remove, and custom JavaScript in any combination.

findReplace.title: Find & Replace
findReplace.desc: Replace text globally or at exact positions. Supports case sensitivity toggle, replace-first or replace-all modes, and positional replacement by character index.

capabilities.c4: Replace from start or end of filename
capabilities.c5: Works on name, extension, or full filename

insert.title: Add / Insert Text
insert.desc: Insert text at the beginning, end, or any character position. Supports dynamic variables like date, time, folder name, and sequence numbers.

capabilities.c1: Insert at start, end, or specific character index
capabilities.c3: Combine with sequence numbers for structured naming
capabilities.c4: Metadata variables: EXIF date, camera model, audio tags
capabilities.c5: Stack with other rules for complex prefixes/suffixes

insert.bestFor: Adding date prefixes, version tags, or structured naming

sequence.title: Auto Sequence
sequence.desc: Add sequential numbering with complete control over type, start value, step, padding, scope, and sorting. Supports numeric, alphabetic, and roman numeral sequences.

capabilities.c1: Numeric, alphabetic (A-Z), and roman numeral sequences
capabilities.c2: Custom start value, step size, and zero-padding
capabilities.c3: Scope: global, per-folder, per-extension, or per-category
capabilities.c4: Sort by name, size, date, or extension before numbering

sequence.bestFor: Organizing large batches with consistent numbering

caseStyle.title: Case & Style Conversion
caseStyle.desc: Convert filenames between 8 case styles including developer-friendly formats. Combine case changes with separator transforms for complete naming convention overhauls.

capabilities.c1: UPPERCASE, lowercase, Title Case, Sentence case
capabilities.c2: Developer styles: camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case, snake_case
capabilities.c4: Combine case + separator in a single operation
capabilities.c5: Apply to name only, extension only, or full filename

regex.title: Regex Patterns
regex.desc: Full regular expression support with capture groups, backreferences, and flag control. The most powerful rule type for complex pattern transformations.

capabilities.c1: Full JavaScript regex with capture groups ($1, $2...)
capabilities.c4: Remove patterns: brackets, trailing numbers, special chars
capabilities.c5: Extract and restructure dates, IDs, and metadata

customJs.title: Custom JavaScript
customJs.desc: Write your own transformation function with full access to filename, extension, index, file size, and modification time. For when built-in rules aren't enough.

capabilities.c1: Full JavaScript function with rename(options) API
capabilities.c2: Access: filename, extension, index, size, modifiedTime
capabilities.c3: Built-in code editor with syntax highlighting
capabilities.c5: AI prompt generator — copy to ChatGPT/Claude for code help

removeCleanup.title: Remove & Cleanup
removeCleanup.desc: Remove characters by count, range, or type. Quick-cleanup mode strips digits, symbols, spaces, Chinese, or English characters in one click.

capabilities.c1: Remove N characters from start or end
capabilities.c2: Remove characters within a specific index range
capabilities.c3: Quick cleanup: strip digits, symbols, spaces, Chinese, or English
capabilities.c4: Multi-select: combine cleanup types in one pass
capabilities.c5: Scope control: apply to name, extension, or full filename

removeCleanup.bestFor: Stripping unwanted characters and file clutter

preview.title:
See every change
before it happens
preview.desc: No guessing, no accidents. Every rule you add updates the preview instantly. See original names, new names, and any conflicts — all in real time.

livePreview.title: Instant Live Preview
livePreview.desc: Changes appear as you type. Every rule modification updates the entire file list immediately — no refresh button, no waiting.

conflictDetection.title: Conflict Detection
conflictDetection.desc: Automatically detects when two files would get the same name, preventing data loss. Conflicts are highlighted in red with auto-fix available.

filterViews.title: Smart Filter Views
filterViews.desc: Toggle between showing all files, only affected files, or only conflicts. Focus on what matters at each stage of your workflow.

extensionControl.title: Extension Scope Control
extensionControl.desc: Apply rules to the filename only, the extension only, or the full filename. Fine-grained control prevents accidentally changing file types.

undoRedo.title: Undo & Redo
undoRedo.desc: Built-in undo and redo support. Made a mistake after executing? Revert the entire batch with one click.

oneClickExecute.title: One-Click Execute
oneClickExecute.desc: When the preview looks right, execute all renames directly — files are renamed on your device via the File System Access API.

advancedFilter.title: Advanced File Filtering
advancedFilter.desc: Filter files by name, extension, size, or modification date using conditions like contains, starts with, regex match, greater than, and more. Combine with AND/OR logic.

sorting.title: Flexible Sorting
sorting.desc: Sort files by import order, name (A-Z/Z-A), or extension before renaming. Natural sort ensures proper ordering (1, 2, 10 instead of 1, 10, 2).

automation.title:
Save time with
templates & automation
automation.desc: From one-click presets to intelligent file detection, Rename.Tools reduces repetitive work with powerful automation features.

templateLibrary.title: 11 Built-in Templates
templateLibrary.desc: Ready-to-use rule templates for common tasks: remove brackets, add date prefix, format TV shows, clean download names, convert camelCase to kebab-case, and more.

userPresets.title: Custom User Presets
userPresets.desc: Save your rule chains as reusable presets with names, descriptions, tags, and categories. Pin favorites for quick access.

presetManagement.title: Import, Export & Share
presetManagement.desc: Export presets as JSON files, import from colleagues, and generate shareable URLs. Your workflow is fully portable.

intelligentSuggestions.title: Intelligent Suggestions
intelligentSuggestions.desc: Automatically detects file types (photos, music, video series, documents) and suggests optimal rename rules tailored to your files.

mediaScraper.title: TMDb Media Scraper
mediaScraper.desc: Match video files with TV show information from The Movie Database. Auto-detect show names, seasons, and episode titles for perfect media library naming.

metadataExtraction.title: Metadata Extraction
metadataExtraction.desc: Read EXIF data from photos (date, camera, resolution) and audio tags from music files (artist, album, track). Use metadata variables in rename templates.

scriptExport.title: Script Export
scriptExport.desc: Export your entire rename operation as Bash, PowerShell, or CSV. Generate undo scripts to reverse any operation. Perfect for CI/CD pipelines or remote servers.

privacy.title:
Your files never leave
your browser
privacy.desc: Rename.Tools is architecturally incapable of accessing your files from a server. Everything runs locally via the File System Access API.

localProcessing.title: 100% Local Processing
localProcessing.desc: All file operations run as client-side JavaScript in your browser. No backend, no API calls for file operations, no server-side processing.

noUpload.title: Zero Upload Architecture
noUpload.desc: Your files are never uploaded anywhere. We use the File System Access API to read and rename files directly on your device.

noRetention.title: No Data Retention
noRetention.desc: Close the tab and everything is gone. No file data is persisted — only your language, theme, and saved presets stay in localStorage.

offlineReady.title: Works Offline
offlineReady.desc: After the initial page load, Rename.Tools works fully offline. No internet connection required for renaming operations.

openSource.title: Fully Open Source
openSource.desc: Every line of code is open on GitHub under AGPL-3.0. Verify our privacy claims yourself — no hidden tracking, no analytics scripts.

freeForever.title: Free Forever
freeForever.desc: No trials, no premium tiers, no paywalls. Every feature is available to everyone. No sign-up required.

features.ctaDesc: Join thousands of users who save hours with Rename.Tools. Free, private, and runs right in your browser.
features.ctaSubtext: Free forever • No sign-up • Works offline

app.title: Batch Rename
app.privacyDesc: All operations run locally in your browser. No uploads, no content reading, cleared on close.
app.browserCompatOk: Your browser supports File System Access API for direct local renaming.
app.browserCompatFail: Your browser doesn't support File System Access API. Use sample test mode or export scripts.
app.preferencesDesc: Configure global application settings and behaviors.
app.kofiTooltip: Small coffee, big motivation. Thanks for the support ☕

tmdb.title: TMDB API Key
tmdb.description: Used for automatic movie/TV show metadata matching. You can get a free API Key at themoviedb.org.

files.title: File List
files.noFiles: No files yet. Import files or folders to start.
files.sampleHint: Enter file names (one per line) to test rules

filter.title: File Filter
filter.noConditions: No filter conditions. Click to add

rules.title: Rule Chain
rules.noRules: No rules yet. Click above to add one.

sequence.preservePatternHint: Use capture group to extract number, e.g. (\d+)

customJs.hint: Define rename(options) function, return new filename
customJs.aiPromptDesc: Copy this template to send to AI (like ChatGPT, Claude) to generate rename code:

photoCleanup.name: Photo file cleanup
photoCleanup.desc: Replace IMG_ with Photo_ and add sequence

lowercaseDash.name: Lowercase + dashes
lowercaseDash.desc: Convert to lowercase and spaces to dashes

tvShowFormat.name: TV show format
tvShowFormat.desc: Format episode numbers as S01E01 - Title

templateEditor.placeholder: Click buttons below to add variables or text...

presets.noPinned: No pinned items. Pin templates or presets for quick access.
presets.noPresets: No saved presets yet. Save your rule chains for reuse.
presets.noPresetsDesc: Presets let you save your favorite rule combinations for quick reuse across different file batches.
presets.howToCreateDesc: Configure rules in the Rules panel, then click the "Save as Preset" button.
presets.howToImportDesc: Click the button below to import previously exported preset files (.json format).
presets.howToCreateSimple: Configure rules in Rules panel, then click "Save as Preset"
presets.howToImportSimple: Or import existing preset files using the button below
presets.saveDescription: Save current {count} rules as a reusable preset

descriptions.photo_collection: Organize photos with date and sequence numbering
descriptions.music_library: Format tracks with artist, album, and track number
descriptions.document_archive: Archive documents with date and sequence prefix
descriptions.video_scrape: Auto-match show names, seasons and episode titles from TMDb

scraper.title: Media Scraper
scraper.description: Match video files with TV show information from TMDb
scraper.noGroups: No TV show patterns detected in the selected video files
scraper.groupsHint: The following shows were detected from file names. Edit titles if needed before searching.

apiKey.title: Configure TMDb API Key
apiKey.description: Media scraping requires a free TMDb API key. Your key is stored locally in the browser only.
apiKey.howToGet: How to get a free API key:
apiKey.step1: Visit themoviedb.org and create a free account
apiKey.step2: Go to Settings → API → Create API Key
apiKey.step3: Copy the API Read Access Token (v4 auth)
apiKey.label: API Read Access Token
apiKey.invalid: Invalid API key. Please check and try again.
apiKey.network: Network error. Please check your connection.
apiKey.timeout: Request timed out. Please try again.
apiKey.localOnly: Your API key is stored locally in browser storage and never sent to our servers.

execute.confirmWarning: Note: Renaming will change the file modification time.
execute.timestampWarning: I understand: renaming will change file modification timestamps
execute.activationRequired: Browser permission expired — click here to continue renaming

about.title: About This Project
about.description: Rename.Tools is a powerful browser-based batch file renaming tool. All operations run locally in your browser for total privacy.

Key features of this Service include: all file processing is performed locally in your browser; we do not collect, store, or transmit any user file data; support for various renaming rules including regular expressions, sequence generation, and case conversion; real-time preview functionality to confirm results before execution.
legal.termsUserConductDesc: When using this Service, you agree to: not use the Service for any illegal purposes; not attempt to damage, interfere with, or bypass the security measures of the Service; not abuse the Service or harm other users' interests in any way; take full responsibility for your use of the Service and its outcomes.

legal.termsLiabilityDesc: Batch renaming operations are typically irreversible. You expressly acknowledge and agree that: you use the Service entirely at your own risk; you are responsible for carefully verifying results through the preview feature before executing rename operations; we strongly recommend backing up important files before any operation; we are not liable for any losses resulting from operational errors.

Any disputes arising from these terms shall first be resolved through friendly negotiation; if negotiation fails, either party may submit the dispute to a court of competent jurisdiction.
legal.termsContactDesc: If you have any questions or suggestions regarding these Terms of Service, please contact us via: GitHub Issues (recommended): https://github.com/chenz24/rename.tools/issues
legal.privacyIntro: We understand how important privacy is to you. This Privacy Policy explains how Rename.Tools handles your information. In short: we don't collect any of your data.
legal.privacyLocalDesc: Rename.Tools uses a purely browser-side architecture, which means: all file operations are performed locally on your device; your files are never uploaded to any server; file contents, file names, and metadata never leave your browser; the Service works offline after initial load.
legal.privacyNoCollectionDesc: We explicitly do not collect: your file contents, file names, or any file metadata; your personal information (name, email, phone, etc.); your device information or browser fingerprint; your IP address or geographic location; your usage behavior or browsing history.
legal.privacyNoStorageDesc: This Service does not persist any user data to remote servers. In your local browser, we only store: language preferences (localStorage); theme settings (localStorage); renaming rule presets you voluntarily save (localStorage). When you close the browser tab, all temporary data (including file lists and current rule configurations) is automatically cleared. You can clear all locally stored data at any time through your browser settings.
legal.privacyCookiesDesc: This Service only uses essential functional cookies to store your language and theme preferences. These cookies: do not contain any personally identifiable information; are not used for tracking or analytics purposes; are not shared with any third parties; can be deleted at any time through your browser settings.
legal.privacyThirdPartyDesc: TMDb API Integration: If you choose to use the media metadata scraping feature, you need to configure a TMDb API key. This key is stored only in your browser's local storage and is not sent to our servers. API calls are made directly from your browser to TMDb servers; we do not participate in or log these communications. Hosting: This website is hosted on Vercel. Vercel may collect standard server logs (such as IP addresses and access times), which are managed by Vercel according to their privacy policy. We do not have access to this data.
legal.privacySecurityDesc: Although we don't store your data, we still take the following measures to protect service security: use HTTPS to encrypt all network communications; regularly update dependencies to fix known security vulnerabilities; open source code subject to community review; no use of any third-party tracking or analytics scripts.
legal.privacyChildrenDesc: This Service is not specifically targeted at children under 13 years of age. Since we do not collect any personal information, there is no issue of collecting children's privacy data.
legal.privacyChangesDesc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect service changes or legal requirements. Any significant changes will be posted on this page. We recommend periodically reviewing this page to stay informed about our privacy practices.
legal.privacyRightsDesc: Since we do not collect or store your personal data, traditional data subject rights (such as access, rectification, deletion) are technically not applicable. However, you can always: clear your browser's local storage to delete all locally saved preferences; choose not to use features that require third-party APIs; review our open source code to verify our privacy claims.
legal.privacyContactDesc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us via GitHub Issues.
legal.disclaimerIntro: Please read this disclaimer carefully before using Rename.Tools. By using this Service, you acknowledge that you understand and accept these terms.
legal.disclaimerGeneralDesc: Rename.Tools is a free open-source tool designed to help users efficiently batch rename files. This tool is provided "as is," and we make no express or implied warranties regarding its completeness, accuracy, or suitability. Use of this tool is entirely at your own risk.
legal.disclaimerRiskDesc: Batch file renaming is an operation with potential risks. Please note: rename operations are typically irreversible and may not be undone once executed; incorrect renaming rules may cause file name confusion or loss of original file organization; renaming certain system files or files that programs depend on may cause system or application failures; on some file systems, changing file names may affect file modification timestamps.
legal.disclaimerBackupDesc: We strongly recommend: performing a complete backup of target files before executing any batch renaming operation; using the preview feature provided by this tool to carefully check expected results; for important files, first verify rule correctness on a test file set; consider using the "Export Undo Script" feature provided by this tool to restore file names if needed.
legal.disclaimerNoWarrantyDesc: To the maximum extent permitted by law: this tool comes with no warranties of any kind, whether express or implied; we do not guarantee that this tool will meet your specific requirements; we do not guarantee that the operation of this tool will be uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free; we do not guarantee that results obtained using this tool will be accurate or reliable; we are not liable for any data loss, file corruption, system failure, or other damages.
legal.disclaimerLiabilityDesc: Under no circumstances shall the developers and contributors of Rename.Tools be liable for: any direct, indirect, incidental, special, punitive, or consequential damages arising from the use or inability to use this tool; any loss of profits, data loss, or business interruption; any third-party claims. These limitations apply even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.
legal.disclaimerOpenSourceDesc: Rename.Tools is open source under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0). This means: you are free to use, copy, modify, and distribute this software; you may use it for commercial or non-commercial purposes; you must retain the copyright notice and license notice in all copies; if you modify and run the software on a network server, you must provide the source code to the users; this software is provided "as is" without any warranty.
legal.disclaimerThirdPartyDesc: This tool may contain or link to third-party content, libraries, or services. We are not responsible for the accuracy, completeness, or availability of any third-party content. When using third-party services (such as TMDb API), you must also comply with the terms of service of those services.
legal.disclaimerJurisdictionDesc: This disclaimer is governed by applicable laws. If any part of this statement is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining parts shall remain in effect.
legal.disclaimerAcknowledgmentDesc: By using Rename.Tools, you acknowledge that: you have read and understood this disclaimer; you agree to be bound by this disclaimer; you understand the risks of batch renaming operations; you will be responsible for your use of this tool and its outcomes.
